level_signals: Own campaign planning, budget allocation, optimization, and performance tracking end-to-end
Building new channels or programs from scratch
Hands-on experience managing mobile acquisition campaigns

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have hands-on experience managing mobile acquisition campaigns across platforms like Meta, TikTok, Apple Search Ads, or Google App Campaigns
Think beyond installs and optimize for full-funnel metrics like activation, retention, LTV, and revenue
Are comfortable working within mobile attribution constraints (SKAN, MMPs) and making decisions with imperfect data
Are comfortable operating in ambiguity and building new channels or programs from scratch
Collaborate effectively with creative, product, lifecycle, and data teams to drive growth
Take ownership and move quickly, balancing experimentation with execution
Communicate clearly and can translate performance into business outcomes (growth, revenue, efficiency)

scope.surface: Mobile app acquisition and growth channels (installs, activation, retention, monetization)
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B AI product — consumer app + enterprise (SaaS/usage-based AI audio/voice)
core_responsibilities: Develop and execute mobile growth strategies across key acquisition channels (Meta, TikTok, Apple Search Ads, Google App Campaigns, and emerging platforms)
Own campaign planning, budget allocation, optimization, and performance tracking end-to-end
Partner closely with creative teams to develop high-performing, platform-native ad creative (static, video, UGC)
Design and run experiments to improve install rate, activation, retention, and monetization
Work with product and lifecycle teams to optimize onboarding, conversion flows, and downstream engagement
Build and leverage attribution frameworks (SKAN, MMPs) to measure true performance and guide decision-making
Analyze performance and optimize campaigns based on CPI, CAC, LTV, payback period, and retention metrics
Identify and scale high-performing channels while quickly cutting underperforming ones
Stay up to date with platform changes, privacy updates, and emerging opportunities in mobile growth

green_flags: Fully remote-global role with optional office access in multiple major cities
High-growth company with $781M raised and $11B valuation — significant resources for paid acquisition
Explicit mandate to 'scale significantly' mobile growth — greenfield ownership opportunity
Annual learning & development stipend and social travel stipend mentioned
Lean autonomous team structure with minimal bureaucracy
Annual company offsite for entire team
red_flags: No years of experience specified — seniority expectations are ambiguous
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for non-local candidates
No job titles policy ('We don't have job titles') may make leveling and career progression opaque
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent — may indicate very high baseline expectations with little flexibility

scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product platforms)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; listed verbatim from JD)

ambiguities: The JD says 'We don't have job titles' in the How we work section, yet the role posting itself carries the title 'Mobile Growth Manager' — may indicate this is an external label only.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere; work_auth set to 'open' and visa_sponsorship set to 'unclear' as defaults.
No explicit 'nice to haves' section exists; all listed requirements treated as hard must-haves based on the unconditional 'You'll thrive with us if you:' framing.
